seed_instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals.
org_constraint: Ensure that the keywords "research opportunities" and "collaboration" appear in the second paragraph.
new_constraint: The keyword "opportunity" must be used exclusively in its singular form.
instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals. Ensure that the keywords "research opportunities" and "collaboration" appear in the second paragraph. The keyword "opportunity" must be used exclusively in its singular form.
response:
Subject: Introduction and Interest in [Professor's Research Area] - [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

My name is [Your Name], and I am a [Your Year, e.g., second-year undergraduate] student majoring in [Your Major] here at [Your University]. I have been following your work on [Mention a specific paper, project, or area of their research] with great interest, as it aligns closely with my growing academic focus on [Mention your specific area of interest].

I am writing to express my enthusiasm for the possibility of engaging with your laboratory or projects. I am particularly keen to explore potential research opportunities within your field. I am eager to learn more about how my skills in [Mention 1-2 relevant skills, e.g., data analysis, laboratory techniques, programming] could support your ongoing work and I am very open to the prospect of collaboration on future projects.

I have attached my resume/CV for your review, which provides more detail on my academic background and relevant coursework. I would be grateful for the chance to meet with you briefly during your office hours or at another convenient time to discuss your current projects and how I might best contribute to your work.

Thank you very much for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number]
[Your Email Address]
[Your Phone Number (Optional)]<turn|>
